112175.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

112179.explanation: A fireplace, wood stove, or fire basket provides warmth and coziness, but can also cause wood-burning nuisance for neighbors. In Deventer, we set rules for wood burning and address those causing nuisance.

112181.explanation: Trash, old mattresses, and other rubbish in the streets are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This reduces hassle and results in cleaner streets. It also prevents dumping in nature and rural areas.

112189.explanation: BOA officers are extremely important for (the feeling of) safety on the streets. We invest in the Supervision and Enforcement Team to detect and tackle nuisance, vandalism, and (environmental) crime faster. We pay extra attention to the safety of women and girls.

112190.explanation: Feeling safe on the streets is important. Cameras can help with this, but are not always the right solution. We use public space camera surveillance cautiously: preferably as a temporary measure, and residents must be informed where cameras are installed.

112192.explanation: The monopoly on the use of force must remain with the police. Further arming does not automatically lead to more safety. It actually increases the risk of escalation.

112193.explanation: A starter's loan sounds sympathetic but is not proven effective. It can even further drive up housing prices. Additionally, first-time home buyers already have tax advantages. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest the money in quickly building affordable housing.

112194.explanation: Many groups already have priority for social rental housing. Giving more people priority only makes waiting times longer. By increasing social housing to 40% in Deventer, we will reduce waiting lists.

112198.explanation: People are not on social assistance because they do not want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. From such a calm situation, they can then contribute again to our society.

112200.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to prevent people from falling into the poverty trap because they earn just too much to qualify for financial support or debt counseling.

112206.explanation: A caregiver who matches your faith, culture, or beliefs can remove barriers and make access to care easier.

112208.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants free menstrual products available in schools, libraries, community centers, and government buildings.

112209.explanation: No party is inherently in favor of raising taxes for residents. However, the municipality’s financial situation does not allow for lowering taxes such as property tax.

112211.explanation: Although the tourist tax in Deventer is relatively low, raising it is not necessary. GroenLinks-PvdA would like to explore how the tax can be used to promote sustainability and culture in Deventer.

112212.explanation: Deventer will grow significantly in the coming years. Therefore, new work areas are also needed. At the same time, we will better utilize existing business parks and ensure sustainable and social employment.

112214.explanation: Integration is difficult if you have to stay unnecessarily long in an asylum seekers' center. That’s why we continue to give priority to status holders, as well as other urgent seekers. Think of victims of domestic violence, house fires, or people returning from care institutions. And we are building more social housing.

112215.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

112217.explanation: Deventers is a unique dialect of Low Saxon, the regional language spoken in Eastern Netherlands. To prevent this language variant from dying out, it is good to teach our children about it.

112218.explanation: In line with Deventer’s population growth, we are open to a fifth coffeeshop in Deventer. We protect our youth against drugs through education and by not allowing coffeeshops near educational institutions.

112222.explanation: The historic city center of Deventer is best suited for cyclists and pedestrians. Therefore, we want to discourage coming to the center by car. This can be done by raising parking fees. Of course, we will continue to make exceptions for vehicles of disabled people.

112223.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants the streets of Deventer to be designed for people, not cars. We prioritize pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport. More parking spaces in neighborhoods are only relevant if more homes are built.

112225.explanation: Deventer does what is necessary. We take a fair share of asylum seekers and refugees. We continue to hold municipalities in the region accountable for their responsibility to also accommodate people. No one sleeps on the street in Deventer.

112226.explanation: The Burgerweeshuis and the Schouwburg will both retain their cultural identity in the future. Therefore, there is no question of a merger. Currently, the municipality is investigating cooperation at one location. Burgerweeshuis and Schouwburg decide about their own future.

112227.explanation: Deventer will build 11,000 homes by 2035. This is urgently needed to tackle the housing shortage and keep our municipality strong. According to GroenLinks-PvdA, 11,000 is not a maximum. We continue to monitor what is needed: additional housing construction is not excluded.

112228.explanation: Artists, musicians, and creative entrepreneurs get affordable work and rehearsal spaces. As a municipality, we actively consider reuse of buildings and cost-covering rents, so creativity can thrive.

112229.explanation: A third bridge over the IJssel is only considered if the necessity is demonstrated. All scenarios and alternatives will be investigated. We want to first improve walking and cycling connections on the existing bridges.

112230.explanation: Go Ahead Eagles plays an extremely important role in Deventer society. It connects us and makes us proud. The municipality can make good arrangements for renovating the stadium, for example through a loan.

112231.explanation: Deventer builds many homes outside the current center. To prevent traffic nuisance, we take a neutral stance towards allowing coffeeshops outside the center, for example in the business park. Of course, we do not want coffeeshops near schools and child centers.

112232.explanation: De Welle offers great opportunities for a new car-reduced city boulevard. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to create a boulevard with space for pedestrians and cyclists. Cars of residents and electric deliveries are welcome guests.

112233.explanation: Eicas is one of many cultural institutions in Deventer. It is too early to discuss structural subsidies for Eicas now. In the future, we will look at what is needed to keep this valuable museum in Deventer.

112175.explanation: Suitability, quality, and experience weigh more heavily than equalizing the number of men and women.

112179.explanation: Allowed within built-up areas under conditions, such as suitable weather, dry and clean wood, respecting stove alerts, having a good chimney, and proper maintenance, etc. No restrictions in rural areas.

112181.explanation: Residents are responsible for this themselves. Annually, up to 300 kilos of bulky waste can be brought free of charge to the Recycling Center with the environmental pass, so waste disposal fees do not unnecessarily increase.

112189.explanation: Gemeentebelang wants safe neighborhoods. BOAs are part of that, but more BOAs is not automatically better. We choose customized approaches: looking at where problems are, how BOAs and police cooperate, and what residents need. Only then do we decide if expansion is needed.

112190.explanation: To properly maintain public order and safety, temporary additional camera surveillance may be necessary. Think of large events or risk matches of Go Ahead Eagles.

112192.explanation: The use of force tools is a task of the police. BOAs mainly need good training and safe equipment, such as body cameras.

112193.explanation: Gemeentebelang wants to help starters. Therefore, we find it logical to explore whether a starter’s loan can assist.

112194.explanation: We want to give residents of our own municipality priority, but within national rules.

112198.explanation: The principle is that people receiving social assistance benefits do socially meaningful work. This should always be according to their ability, as there is also a group for whom this is not feasible.

112200.explanation: Work must pay off; therefore, there must always be an incentive to start working. Support should be aimed at helping people out of poverty, not keeping them in it. With extra attention for children, so they do not become the victims of poverty.

112206.explanation: Care must be equal and accessible for everyone. Freedom of choice is allowed, but the quality of care remains the most important. Care providers must always meet professional quality standards.

112208.explanation: The municipality supports and facilitates free menstrual product distribution points via local partners and the Poverty Fund. In this way, we ensure that girls and women are not hindered by period poverty and can participate normally.

112209.explanation: It would be great if the property tax (ozb) could be lowered, but that is not realistic at the moment. As is well known, Gemeentebelang does everything possible to keep the increase as low as possible. As soon as we see opportunities to reduce the ozb, we will do everything to realize this.

112211.explanation: To remain attractive to tourists, we will not increase the tourist tax. Moreover, the sector has recently already had to absorb a VAT increase.

112212.explanation: Deventer is growing. To keep living, working, and facilities in balance, new business parks are also needed. This keeps our local economy healthy and future-proof.

112214.explanation: Status holders must have a place in our society, but not at the expense of other housing seekers. The real solution is more construction.

112215.explanation: Agricultural land must be preserved for food production. Therefore, we do not want solar panels on agricultural land.

112217.explanation: The Deventer dialect, Sallands, and other dialects deserve attention, but it is up to schools themselves to decide if and how they offer this. Other ways to offer it, such as cultural projects or collaboration with the library, are worth investigating.

112218.explanation: New coffeeshops are possible under conditions. Only in appropriate locations with the right balance between demand, safety, and livability.

112222.explanation: Our city center must remain attractive for people who come shopping by car. Overpriced parking spaces deter visitors and that affects our entrepreneurs. Gemeentebelang wants an accessible and lively center.

112223.explanation: Parking in front of the door is not obvious everywhere, but we must ensure that residents can park their cars properly and nearby. What is needed differs per neighborhood. Sometimes a spot on the edge of the neighborhood for parking cars, sometimes better arrangements in the street.

112225.explanation: Deventer takes its responsibility and meets the legal quotas. In emergencies, we help with temporary shelter. For (new) locations, the neighborhood must always be well involved, and we must consider the support and capacity of our residents.

112226.explanation: We want one house for performing arts on Keizerstraat, where the Theater and the Burgerweeshuis cooperate. Without barriers, with modern innovations, and preserving everyone’s own identity. An open, lively building where something is always happening.

112227.explanation: Deventer will grow considerably. Existing residential areas must be used smarter and better for housing. New residential locations must be sought carefully. We must build enough affordable homes in a pleasant, green, and livable environment, with good facilities nearby.

112228.explanation: Deventer already invests in workplaces for artists. Together with creators, we make a plan and have set aside money for an initial financial contribution to secure space for studios and creators’ spots. After that, we must look at what else is needed and possible.

112229.explanation: Deventer and the region are growing rapidly in terms of housing, work, and facilities. Without a third IJssel bridge, the city will become more congested, which affects the livability and accessibility of the entire municipality and region.

112230.explanation: The municipality can help with a loan under conditions. This increases the feasibility of the renovation.

112231.explanation: We want to look for locations for coffeeshops outside the center, for example on business parks. New locations must never come at the expense of livability and safety in the neighborhood.

112232.explanation: The Welle can only become low-traffic when the flow on the Hanzetracé improves or the third IJssel bridge is built. If we do it now, Deventer will become completely congested and many residents will suffer nuisance. For residents and businesses in the area, car-free is not an option; low-traffic is.

112233.explanation: Deventer has already helped Eicas once to get its basics in order. The museum itself indicated at the start it could do without subsidy. Further structural support sets a precedent and means cutting elsewhere in the cultural budget.

112175.explanation: D66 considers it important that representation in the council is as broad as possible. It is an ambition but not a strict precondition.

112179.explanation: D66 believes open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed. At the same time, we find it important that people heat responsibly, with attention to health, clean air, and neighbors. Therefore, we focus on clear information and compliance with the burning guide.

112181.explanation: D66 believes bulky waste should be able to be disposed of free of charge, but not by default at home. By keeping self-delivery free and only deploying collection where necessary, waste charges remain fair and affordable for everyone.

112189.explanation: D66 values safety. Instead of routinely deploying more enforcement officers, we look at what really works: targeted deployment, cooperation with police, and prevention. This way we strengthen safety without unnecessary expansion.

112190.explanation: D66 stands for safety and privacy. Camera surveillance is a serious measure that belongs only in exceptional situations. We prefer to invest in measures that increase safety without constantly monitoring everyone.

112192.explanation: D66 stands for safety and restrained use of force. A standard baton for enforcement officers does not fit in. Enforcement requires de-escalation, professionalism, and cooperation. Only with demonstrable necessity and clear safeguards can local customization occur. This is not currently the case in Deventer. Should it be convincingly demonstrated in the future, we are willing to carefully discuss it again.

112193.explanation: D66 wants to genuinely help starters to find affordable housing. A municipal starter loan seems attractive but often drives house prices up further. Therefore, we focus on more affordable new construction and national schemes that are clear and fair for everyone.

112194.explanation: D66 believes it is important that people living in Deventer or having a clear bond with it also have a chance at social rental housing. D66 Deventer has worked hard for this in recent periods. This ensures fair distribution and strong, stable neighborhoods.

112198.explanation: D66 does not believe that residents on benefits should be helped with punitive measures. Rather with education, guidance, and support towards work and independence. Some people on benefits are unable to work due to (chronic) illness anyway.

112200.explanation: D66 wants more people to get help with poverty. By raising income limits and simplifying schemes, we prevent people from just missing out. Help must be accessible and understandable.

112206.explanation: D66 believes it is important that people feel seen and respected in care. At the same time, care must remain accessible, affordable, and equal for everyone. Therefore, we choose national rules and quality standards, not local selection based on religion or background.

112208.explanation: D66 states that no one should lack menstrual products due to lack of money. Therefore, we support free provision of tampons and sanitary pads at places where this contributes to health and dignity.

112209.explanation: D66 wants to keep the property tax (ozb) stable and only index it with inflation. This keeps important facilities such as sports, culture, and care affordable and accessible to everyone.

112211.explanation: D66 does not want to increase the tourist tax now. Deventer benefits from visitors, and a stable rate helps entrepreneurs and events. Reconsideration is only possible if additional investments in livability are needed.

112212.explanation: D66 wants Deventer to grow not only in population but also in employment. This requires space for companies, with attention to sustainability, accessibility, and a good balance with housing.

112214.explanation: D66 believes Deventer takes its legal responsibility for housing status holders. It concerns a limited part of social rental homes (approx. 7%) and happens according to clear rules, alongside efforts for extra housing for everyone.

112215.explanation: D66 opposes a ban on solar panels on agricultural land. Due to a provincial decision, Deventer can no longer weigh this itself, which limits local democracy. This increases pressure on wind turbines. A good energy transition requires balance and support.

112217.explanation: D66 values local history and culture but wants schools to have the freedom to shape their own education. It is not up to the municipality to prescribe one fixed program for all schools.

112218.explanation: Deventer is growing and requires a modern and safe coffeeshop policy. By allowing new coffeeshops in well-accessible locations outside the city center and residential neighborhoods, we reduce nuisance, increase control, and ensure better distribution.

112222.explanation: D66 wants street parking to remain more expensive than garage parking so that space is used smartly. There is no reason to increase parking fees further everywhere in the center. We choose guidance rather than additional burdens.

112223.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces in the neighborhoods. D66 wants extra greenery in the neighborhoods where possible, even if that sometimes comes at the cost of parking spots (if feasible).

112225.explanation: D66 follows the distribution law and takes responsibility for humane shelter. Deventer thus makes a fair contribution for people fleeing war and violence. With attention to support and livability in neighborhoods, we ensure good shelter and guidance.

112226.explanation: Disagree. Both organizations are already working together on the project “Performing Arts under One Roof,” and abrupt back-and-forth policies do not contribute to stability and continuity. We choose progress and collaboration to arrive at a future-proof solution.

112227.explanation: D66 supports the construction of a maximum of 11,000 homes to address the housing shortage. This number aligns with current needs and plans. At the same time, we keep an eye on the future: if demand grows further, Deventer must keep space to adjust.

112228.explanation: D66 advocates for more work and living space for artists. Art and culture make Deventer creative, vibrant, and attractive. By investing in affordable studios and workplaces, we give makers room to develop and contribute to the city.

112229.explanation: D66 does not see a need for a third IJssel bridge. It is expensive, damages the natural area, and is mainly intended for a new residential area across the river. It does not solve current accessibility issues. We choose affordable, sustainable solutions within the existing city.

112230.explanation: D66 is proud of Go Ahead Eagles as an important sporting and social pillar of Deventer. We are open to temporary financial support, for example through a lower-interest loan, provided it is responsible and repaid. This way, we support the club and protect public money.

112231.explanation: Deventer is growing and demands a safe and well-regulated coffeeshop policy. Pick-up locations in the city center sometimes cause nuisance. Therefore, D66 also wants to allow coffeeshops at well-accessible locations outside residential areas and the center, with selection based on quality and responsibility.

112232.explanation: D66 wants less through car traffic on the Welle. This will create a safe boulevard for cycling and walking. Residents and visitors can still reach the center by car. The Welle will become a green recreation area and the calling card of Deventer.

112233.explanation: D66 is pleased with EICAS as a valuable cultural asset for Deventer. We follow the museum’s development with interest, but structural financial support from the municipality is not currently on the agenda. D66 attaches great value to culture. If financial support is needed in the future, the party will not rule it out but will engage in open dialogue.

112175.explanation: The VVD provides the best candidate for the position. The party does not decide on candidates from other parties; therefore, the final distribution in the executive board is coincidental. It is already certain that the current mayor is a man.

112179.explanation: The VVD is against a general ban on wood burning. The party sees the use of wood stoves and fire baskets as a matter of personal freedom and responsibility and considers a ban too far-reaching and patronizing.

112181.explanation: The VVD wants to make waste collection cheaper by switching to post-sorting instead of the current differential tariff system with multiple containers and rates. You bring bulky waste yourself, keeping the fixed fee per household low.

112189.explanation: The VVD believes that the capacity of the Supervision & Enforcement Team must be expanded to better tackle nuisance, vandalism, and destruction. The party also wants more visible police presence in all neighborhoods and villages, especially in the evenings and at night.

112190.explanation: The VVD considers smart camera surveillance necessary in places with a lot of nuisance and crime, such as Beestenmarkt, Polstraat, the station, and Boreelplein, because officers and enforcers cannot be everywhere at once.

112192.explanation: VVD Deventer believes that BOAs (Public Order Officers) should be equipped with batons and possibly pepper spray. According to the party, this is necessary to increase their safety and enable them to defend themselves against increasing aggression and violence in public space.

112193.explanation: VVD Deventer supports a municipal starter loan for people buying a house for the first time. The party wants to (re)introduce this scheme to assist starters in the tight housing market with high house prices.

112194.explanation: The VVD prioritizes local residents where possible (for example with new builds or starters) but views regional cooperation as pragmatic and not a strict obligation for all social rental allocations to avoid market disruption. They prefer solutions like building more and starter schemes.

112198.explanation: VVD Deventer believes that people on social assistance should give something back. By performing socially useful work, they remain active, gain structure, meet others, and contribute to society.

112200.explanation: VVD Deventer believes national poverty standards should be followed and income limits not increased locally. Budgets, subsidies, and reserves must be used responsibly, tax money spent carefully, and local charges kept as low as possible.

112206.explanation: The VVD believes healthcare providers may refuse patients who reject them for cultural or religious reasons. The party chooses safety and freedom for healthcare personnel and believes patients do not have the right to refuse healthcare providers based on origin, gender, or religion.

112208.explanation: The VVD finds period poverty unacceptable but wants to address it through general poverty policy and existing assistance. The party does not support separate free provisions by the municipality, as this does not solve the underlying poverty problem and is logistically complicated.

112209.explanation: The VVD focuses on responsible and transparent management of municipal funds. Local taxes, such as property tax and waste collection fees, are lowered where possible. In case of deficits, the municipality first cuts its own costs.

112211.explanation: The VVD accepts tourist tax if the proceeds are directly used for tourist facilities. The party sees increases as harmful to recreation, hospitality, employment, and the city’s tourist appeal.

112212.explanation: A strong business climate attracts companies and retains jobs. VVD invests in 100 hectares of new industrial sites for manufacturing and knowledge economy and modernizes existing locations. Rapid permits without unnecessary delays for entrepreneurs.

112214.explanation: The VVD believes status holders should be placed on the regular waiting list like other housing seekers. This keeps the system fair and maintains support. Giving priority does not fit their vision on the housing crisis and asylum policy.

112215.explanation: The VVD does not see a mandatory ban as necessary and does not exclude exceptions. The party strongly prefers solar panels on roofs and other locations to protect agricultural land. A complete municipal ban does not fit their vision of a flexible, market-driven transition.

112217.explanation: "Reading and writing in the Deventer dialect, we don't do that in Deventer schools. We do that comfortably at home."

112218.explanation: The VVD wants coffeeshops in Deventer to be located far from schools and not in prime city center locations like Nieuwe Markt, to preserve the attractive character of the city.

112222.explanation: The VVD wants affordable parking, with revenues used for better accessibility. The party advocates for more parking garages, smart information on free spots, better bicycle parking, and more flexible parking standards.

112223.explanation: VVD Deventer believes there must be sufficient parking for residents and visitors. Parking problems in neighborhoods must be actively addressed. More parking spaces are needed where demand is high, but with preservation of greenery, space, and livability.

112225.explanation: Due to national and European measures, the number of asylum seekers is expected to decline. VVD Deventer believes Deventer already provides sufficient shelter under the Redistribution Law. Support and shelter for rejected and expunged foreigners will stop, leading to a further decrease.

112226.explanation: VVD Deventer sees one joint location for the Theater and the Burgerweeshuis as more efficient than two expensive buildings. Being under one roof lowers costs and ensures better cooperation, greater audience reach, and a strong cultural provision within limited resources.

112227.explanation: Affordable housing is a top priority. The VVD wants 10,000 homes before 2034 and another 10,000 by 2050, including across the IJssel. Construction will take place inside and outside the city, in cooperation with neighboring municipalities, while preserving greenery and landscape.

112228.explanation: The VVD sees art and culture as important for the living environment and economy. Institutions and creators should earn as much income as possible themselves; subsidies are supplementary. Workplaces and studios can temporarily be housed in existing buildings, with shared use and a businesslike approach.

112229.explanation: The VVD believes that building across the IJssel requires a third bridge. This is important for good accessibility and strengthens Deventer’s and the region’s economic position, with a connection to the A50 offering additional opportunities.

112230.explanation: VVD Deventer supports Go Ahead Eagles only under strict conditions. Previously, a repayable loan was chosen instead of a subsidy. Conversion to a guarantee is negotiable if the financial risk is limited, the business case is sound, and the club invests itself.

112231.explanation: VVD Deventer believes the number of coffeeshops should remain limited. Safety, preventing nuisance, and livability in neighborhoods are paramount. Therefore, the party chooses a few well-regulated coffeeshops in or around the center and not a broad expansion to other locations.

112232.explanation: The VVD believes that the biggest traffic bottlenecks should be tackled first, such as the bottleneck at Van Oldenielstraat and the access to De Vijfhoek. Only after widening the Hanzetracé can Welle and IJsselkade become low-traffic areas.

112233.explanation: The VVD believes that cultural entrepreneurship should be rewarded. An entrepreneurial museum like EICAS, which largely relies on volunteers, can receive structural support provided it sustains itself with its own income and contributes to the economic strengthening of the city center.

112175.explanation: Deventer Belang values equal opportunities but selects leaders based on quality, involvement, and knowledge of Deventer. A mandatory 50/50 quota could exclude good candidates. Diversity is desirable but should not be a strict requirement above expertise and local support.

112179.explanation: Deventer Belang continues to allow fireplaces/wood stoves. They belong to many households and to the character of our villages. We focus on proper use and information to limit smoke nuisance. Banning them solves little and affects residents; wise use works better for livability and health.

112181.explanation: Deventer Belang supports free bulky waste collection at home. This lowers thresholds, prevents dumping, and keeps streets and villages cleaner. A neat living environment is a core municipal task and helps reduce nuisance and cleanup costs in the long term.

112189.explanation: More enforcement officers (boa’s) increase visibility and approachability, help prevent nuisance, and strengthen the sense of security in neighborhoods and villages. Deventer Belang opts for targeted deployment where needed.

112190.explanation: Camera surveillance can help at nuisance and crime hotspots but is not a standard solution. Deventer Belang chooses targeted, temporary, and proportional use, while preserving privacy and livability.

112192.explanation: Deventer Belang considers the safety of enforcement officers important but is cautious about standard arming. A baton may be discussed after risk analysis, training, and clear deployment criteria at nuisance/crime locations. Professional conduct and safety come first, no symbolic politics.

112193.explanation: Deventer Belang believes young people/starters should be able to stay here. A starter’s loan helps provide a fair chance in the local housing market, lowers barriers, and supports purchasing power without harming livability. Practical support for Deventenaren building their future here.

112194.explanation: Deventer Belang believes social rental housing is primarily intended for Deventenaren. Priority for the local municipality helps prevent displacement and strengthens livable, balanced neighborhoods and villages. We choose fair local housing policies that consider capacity and coherence.

112198.explanation: Deventer Belang values social participation, not as a generic obligation. Meaningful social work can be appropriate if aligned with someone's abilities and prospects for paid work. We prefer guidance, tailored opportunities, and reciprocity rather than a one-size-fits-all approach.

112200.explanation: Deventer Belang opts for a broad and fair safety net. More working residents, starters, and families struggle to make ends meet. Raising the income threshold for poverty assistance prevents Deventenaren from falling through the cracks and strengthens the social foundation and livability in Deventer.

112206.explanation: Deventer Belang considers freedom of choice in care important. Appropriate care – including regarding faith, culture, or beliefs – helps build trust and quality. This must be practical and without extra barriers, with respect for diversity and a focus on accessible care that works for everyone in Deventer.

112208.explanation: Deventer Belang finds it important that no one misses out on menstrual products due to poverty but does not support generic free distribution by the municipality. We support targeted, practical poverty and school programs and local partners who assist where it is truly needed in Deventer.

112209.explanation: Deventer Belang advocates for lower property taxes (OZB). Residents, associations, and local entrepreneurs deserve more financial room. Deventer Belang believes the municipality must first work efficiently and make tough choices so that burdens can be lowered without putting livability and core tasks in Deventer under pressure.

112211.explanation: Deventer Belang opposes an increase in tourist tax. We believe extra charges affect visitors and local businesses and make events less attractive. We choose a hospitable municipality with a focus on better distribution and quality of tourism, not on higher taxes.

112212.explanation: Deventer Belang supports new business parks if locally needed for work and economy. Deventer Belang believes integration must be careful – no generic blocks but quality, local scale, and appearance that fits Deventer.

112214.explanation: Deventer Belang opposes automatic priority for one group. We believe social rental housing is primarily for those locally waiting for a home, such as starters, families, and elderly from Deventer. Refugees deserve fair reception and guidance, but no automatic priority.

112215.explanation: Deventer Belang does not favor a general ban. We think solar panels on agricultural land can sometimes help local energy production and farmer income but only after careful study of integration, soil quality, and community support. Solutions must suit Deventer.

112217.explanation: Deventer Belang values the Deventer dialect as culturally important, but the municipality does not control the curriculum. We choose to stimulate and facilitate, not impose. This way, schools can independently give attention to the Deventer dialect as part of local identity and cohesion in Deventer.

112218.explanation: Deventer Belang opposes allowing new coffeeshops. Deventer Belang believes the current balance between regulated sales, safety, and livability must be maintained. Expansion increases the risk of nuisance and pressure on public order in neighborhoods and centers.

112222.explanation: Deventer Belang opposes more expensive paid parking in the city center. Higher parking costs impact residents, visitors, and local businesses and shift pressure to surrounding neighborhoods and towns. We choose accessibility and smart parking solutions, not standard price increases.

112223.explanation: Deventer Belang opts for customization. Additional parking spaces may be built where pressure is truly high but not at the expense of greenery or traffic safety. First local research and consultation with residents, so solutions fit the neighborhood or village and keep Deventer livable.

112225.explanation: Deventer Belang opposes ‘automatic constant capacity’. Shelters should adjust to capacity and livability in neighborhoods and villages. Deventer has taken responsibility, but balance must be examined locally and periodically. We choose realism and support, not fixed number limits.

112226.explanation: Deventer Belang supports maintaining independence. We have previously advocated for a realistic plan B. The Burgerweeshuis and the Schouwburg must each retain their own strengths and identity. Cooperation is possible, but merging only after research, support, and without cultural risk.

112227.explanation: Deventer Belang rejects a rigid building cap. Building should follow local needs and the capacity of neighborhoods and villages. Numbers must not be goals in themselves. First local research on traffic, greenery, facilities, and livability so new construction strengthens Deventer rather than disrupts it.

112228.explanation: Deventer Belang supports culture with local impact but not automatic budget increases. Additional support for jobs for artists is possible if it strengthens Deventer and is balanced with livability in neighborhoods and villages.

112229.explanation: Deventer Belang advocates for a third bridge over the IJssel. This improves accessibility and distributes traffic pressure more fairly. Realization is only possible after research so the bridge strengthens Deventer without causing damage or nuisance.

112230.explanation: Deventer Belang supports Go Ahead Eagles; they are part of our identity and pride. Deventer Belang is, as in the past, willing to make a municipal loan/guarantee available for stadium renovation, with clear, fair agreements and no open-ended commitments.

112231.explanation: Deventer Belang opposes coffeeshops outside the city center. Distribution increases risks of nuisance and safety issues for neighborhoods and villages. We choose to maintain the current balance, strict enforcement, and protection of livability, not expansion of locations.

112232.explanation: Deventer Belang is against a car-free Welle. The Welle is a historic and functional traffic artery for residents, hospitality, and events. Making it car-free increases pressure on surrounding neighborhoods. We choose balance, safety, and accessibility, not blocking traffic.

112233.explanation: Deventer Belang is critical of museum EICAS in its current form. The budget is increasing and demands growing. Culture may enrich Deventer but not with an open-ended bill.

112175.explanation: In forming a new municipal executive, suitability comes first, but a more balanced distribution between men and women is an important goal.

112179.explanation: The municipality may set requirements such as a combustion guide (RIVM) or combustion alert (NHK).

112181.explanation: We want to continue giving residents the opportunity to bring up to 300 kilos per year to the landfill for free; collection costs money.

112189.explanation: Enforcement officers (boa's) and neighborhood police officers must be more visible and accessible in neighborhoods and villages to jointly promote safety with citizens.

112190.explanation: We want to use this possibility primarily at sensitive locations because cameras work preventively, aid in investigations, and enhance the feeling of safety.

112192.explanation: Both the bodycam and baton have a de-escalating effect and contribute to the personal safety of enforcement officers.

112193.explanation: CDA Deventer wants to help starters; young people often miss out. We are positive about a possible starter’s loan in Deventer if research shows it is effective.

112194.explanation: At the initiative of CDA Deventer, there will be priority in Deventer for home seekers with local ties, for up to 50% of new social rental and affordable owner-occupied homes. We continue to advocate for the use of this measure.

112198.explanation: We believe that everyone should be able to contribute in some way to stay involved and connected to our society. Naturally, this measure requires customization.

112200.explanation: The CDA wants to counteract the poverty trap as much as possible. Carefully raising the poverty threshold can activate people to start working. However, poverty issues are not only about money but also about health, welfare, and participation in society.

112206.explanation: We think this is not feasible given the current pressure on healthcare. Furthermore, we do not believe this fits in a society where connection and solidarity are central.

112208.explanation: Parties in the city currently receive no signals from families about period poverty. In several places (e.g., the Food Bank and Children's Aid), menstrual products such as tampons and pads are sufficiently available. If other signals arise, more distribution may be necessary.

112209.explanation: CDA Deventer wants the property tax (OZB) not to increase, except for inflation correction. However, a lower OZB is not feasible given the financial situation of municipalities due to a smaller national contribution.

112211.explanation: Deventer has positioned itself for years as a hospitable city. We want to remain a tourist-friendly city; tourists contribute to the sustainability of our service level, such as shops. Our tourist tax is in line with the national and regional averages of Overijssel and Gelderland.

112212.explanation: Research has shown that an additional business park is needed due to demand from local (SME) entrepreneurs, primarily intended for the (local) manufacturing industry, which provides employment and local economic benefits. CDA opposes even more large distribution centers.

112214.explanation: Without that priority, bigger problems arise, such as more status holders in a reception center, requiring more accommodation places or ending up homeless. We want status holders to integrate into our society. More social rental homes are also needed for all other target groups.

112215.explanation: To meet climate goals, a good mix of energy from sun and wind is needed. We support green energy projects and believe in cooperation with the community, so sustainable initiatives are locally supported or owned, such as local energy cooperatives.

112217.explanation: We believe the municipality should not interfere with the schools' curriculum.

112218.explanation: There are sufficient coffeeshops for local demand, and Deventer does not want to promote cannabis tourism as a border region.

112222.explanation: In times of increased online shopping, it is important to optimally facilitate the inner city with good accessibility and (affordable) parking. The rates in Deventer have been carefully established in recent years and are adjusted annually in good consultation.

112223.explanation: Parking in the neighborhoods is becoming an increasingly big challenge, but adding extra parking spots is not the solution and conflicts with the desire to further green the city.

112225.explanation: We want to continue taking responsibility according to the Distribution Act, which means the number remains roughly the same. We stand for a fair distribution among municipalities, but good consultation with neighbors is crucial.

112226.explanation: We believe consolidating at one location is a good choice. Both institutions can strengthen each other, the location is ideal relative to public transport, and this way we preserve investment space for other important cultural ambitions, such as space for makers/studios.

112227.explanation: Deventer aims to build 11,500 homes by 2040, and CDA Deventer supports this. We stand for manageable growth in the coming decades so that we can maintain our service level.

112228.explanation: We find it important that Deventer has good and long-term available space for makers/studios.

112229.explanation: With a third IJssel bridge, there will finally be a ring road around the city, which is good for the environment, promotes mobility, makes the Welle car-quiet, and reduces traffic problems around the Wilhelmina bridge.

112230.explanation: We are positive towards expanding the stadium. Go Ahead Eagles and its stadium are a showcase for the municipality of Deventer. The municipality should think along as much as possible to enable the stadium renovation with a loan.

112231.explanation: As far as CDA Deventer is concerned, there will definitely be no more coffeeshops, but we do support one or more of the four coffeeshops outside the center at locations where (traffic) disturbances for residents can be limited.

112232.explanation: CDA wants a car-quiet Welle, not completely car-free. However, the Welle can only become car-quiet if the access for (through) traffic at the top of the city (the Hanzetrace) is upgraded, partly by adjustments to Van Oldenielstraat and a third IJssel bridge.

112233.explanation: CDA is in favor of a museum at the Nieuwe Markt location. We have doubts about the feasibility of the museum in its current form and therefore offer no structural support. With programming that appeals to a broader audience, financial support from the municipality is also an option.

112175.explanation: It concerns the qualities of candidates, preferably with as much proportional distribution as possible.

112179.explanation: Where it does not cause nuisance to the immediate surroundings, it is fine for wood stoves to remain allowed.

112181.explanation: If it is easier for residents, unwanted litter on the streets can be prevented; separating (waste) and proper recycling also becomes more feasible.

112189.explanation: More money must primarily go to the police, which has been severely cut back in recent years.

112190.explanation: Privacy is a great value; camera surveillance does not prevent violence.

112192.explanation: Violence control remains primarily a task for the police.

112193.explanation: This could help starters enormously; reintroducing it would be highly desirable.

112194.explanation: Currently, it's half and half; we find that sufficient.

112198.explanation: It entirely depends on personal circumstances; where possible, efforts should be made here.

112200.explanation: The poverty threshold is already minimal. Also, from a solidarity perspective, the threshold should be as low as possible.

112206.explanation: Professional caregivers are expected to provide care regardless of origin, religion, or belief.

112208.explanation: However, this should not be necessary.

112209.explanation: The contribution from the national government is far too low for all the tasks that have been transferred to municipalities in recent years.

112211.explanation: Only if tourism increases to such an extent that additional costs need to be incurred.

112212.explanation: There are plenty of possibilities; do not forget that the A1 business park stood almost completely empty for years.

112214.explanation: In practice, this is already the case for all urgent groups.

112215.explanation: The province is responsible for this. Since January 1, 2024, this is no longer possible.

112217.explanation: Paying attention can do no harm.

112218.explanation: Given the expected growth in our population, this is desirable.

112222.explanation: Space in our beautiful city center is limited and will not increase. Therefore, discouraging (unnecessary) car traffic to the center is a great way to keep the center accessible and livable.

112223.explanation: The municipality could better consider parking hubs on the outskirts of Deventer.

112225.explanation: If necessary and possible, more or fewer asylum seekers should be accommodated.

112226.explanation: The combination will never work; moreover, the estimated costs will be far exceeded.

112227.explanation: Space is limited both in Deventer itself and beyond.

112228.explanation: There are plenty of beautiful locations that are currently vacant and could be used well at an affordable price.

112229.explanation: Not needed for the time being, and the question is what that will mean for the accessibility of the city and surrounding areas.

112230.explanation: If it concerns loans, we think that is fine given the importance of Kowet.

112231.explanation: As long as the conditions for establishment are met, we support it.

112232.explanation: De Welle is an important through-route for cars for which it will be difficult to find an alternative. Many streets in the center are already car-free or car-reduced. At the moment, this still provides sufficient space for everyone.

112233.explanation: Museums are important for visitors to Deventer, and community funds may certainly be allocated to them.

112175.explanation: The Christian Union primarily assesses candidate aldermen on quality. It is up to the coalition parties to nominate aldermen on behalf of their party. For a good balance in the executive board, we find a mix of men and women desirable but do not enforce it.

112179.explanation: The Christian Union stands for a healthy living environment for everyone. Wood stoves and open fireplaces cause nuisance and health complaints due to fine particles. This worsens with poor burning and calm weather. To protect health, the municipality sets rules for wood burning.

112181.explanation: The Christian Union supports the 'polluter pays' principle. Everyone can bring their waste free of charge to the Westfalenstraat. The costs of collecting bulky waste are charged. We do support arrangements for special groups such as the elderly who cannot bring their waste themselves.

112189.explanation: The Christian Union values an open and safe society. We have enough enforcement officers. There is no need to expand capacity now.

112190.explanation: For the Christian Union, safety on the streets is important. Camera surveillance creates an illusion of safety. Real safety is achieved with police officers and enforcement officers visibly present in the city center, neighborhoods, and districts.

112192.explanation: The Christian Union supports safety on the streets. Enforcement (the BOAs) is important for that. They currently carry out their tasks without problems and without batons. If more is needed, the police are always nearby. Therefore, we do not consider batons necessary.

112193.explanation: The Christian Union finds it important that there are enough affordable homes for starters. We want the municipality to impose this as a requirement in housing plans. Additional measures are also needed, such as a starter loan for those without parents who can co-finance.

112194.explanation: The Christian Union believes that everyone with a low income should have a chance at social rental housing. By only granting priority when truly necessary, we keep the group of freely allocable social rental homes as large as possible.

112198.explanation: For the Christian Union, the starting point is that everyone has talents to contribute to society. We want the municipality to stimulate active participation in society (without coercion). Volunteer work is as valuable as paid work.

112200.explanation: The Christian Union wants to fight poverty. The income limit for support may be increased so that every family can get by.

112206.explanation: For the Christian Union, freedom of choice in healthcare is very important. It is nice to be helped by someone who shares your values. We therefore want everyone to be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits them.

112208.explanation: The Christian Union wants everyone to participate in society. Some women and girls, due to low income or other reasons, do not have access to tampons and sanitary pads. The Christian Union wants the municipality to support the organizations that provide these for free.

112209.explanation: The Christian Union stands for financially healthy policy. The municipality of Deventer has ambitions to grow as a city. Building new homes and roads costs money. If we want to realize the ambitions, there is no room to lower the property tax (OZB).

112211.explanation: The Christian Union supports raising the tourist tax. Deventer applies low rates compared to other cities of similar size. Deventer attracts more tourists. With a higher tax, we can benefit more and reinvest in Deventer.

112212.explanation: The Christian Union wants sufficient places to work in Deventer. But, as with housing, we prefer infill to expansion. This means we first choose to redevelop existing industrial areas.

112214.explanation: The Christian Union supports rapid integration of refugees with a residence permit. If they get housing quickly, they can start integration sooner and rebuild their lives.

112215.explanation: The Christian Union wants a transition to sustainable energy supply. For this, we need solar panels and wind energy. We place solar panels mostly on business roofs first, but we also need solar fields. Using agricultural land is not taboo in this.

112217.explanation: The Christian Union supports the preservation of tangible and intangible heritage. However, the Deventers dialect is not a clearly defined language. We should also not overload education with extra tasks. The focus should be on good education.

112218.explanation: The Christian Union finds the current four coffeeshops more than enough. Drug use causes problems with health, addiction, and illegal cultivation. To protect young people, we do not want coffeeshops near schools or along cycling routes.

112222.explanation: The Christian Union wants to promote sustainable transport such as cycling and public transport. However, it is also important that the city center is accessible for large shopping trips. Paid parking rates should cover the costs of parking. Indexation is fine, but no extra increase is necessary.

112223.explanation: The Christian Union supports green neighborhoods and sustainable modes of transport. That way we keep our air clean and ourselves and our children healthy and safe.

112225.explanation: The Christian Union wants us to contribute to the sheltering of refugees. In the AZC in Schalkhaar and several temporary locations, we provide humane shelter without major problems. We stand in solidarity with Ter Apel, so closure is not an option.

112226.explanation: The Christian Union opposed the decision to bring the pop stage and theater under one roof. We want to give the current process a chance but will monitor it critically. We see major risks in the merger. If necessary, we will stop the project to prevent a second Viking debacle.

112227.explanation: The Christian Union wants to build for the needs of Deventer. We also want to contribute to solving the national housing shortage. It is already challenging to build so many homes in a short time. So raising building heights is not very realistic, but is also not taboo.

112228.explanation: The Christian Union sees the added value of artists and other creative professions. In recent years, much temporary housing has been demolished or repurposed for homes. The municipality must actively seek space for new workplaces in consultation with developers.

112229.explanation: There has never been thorough research into the necessity of a third IJssel bridge. Before we start lobbying for the bridge, the Christian Union needs to be clear about what it will cost us (money, nature, extra traffic) and what it will yield (accessibility).

112230.explanation: The Christian Union Deventer is proud of our football club. Go Ahead Eagles is capable of raising sufficient funds, among other things through sponsors. The Christian Union finds it more important that money goes to sports clubs for everyone.

112231.explanation: The Christian Union finds the current four coffeeshops more than enough. Drug use causes problems with health, addiction, and illegal cultivation. To protect young people, we do not want coffeeshops near schools or along cycling routes.

112232.explanation: By making De Welle car-reduced and eventually car-free, the Christian Union wants to create a better connection between the IJssel and the city center. Deventer will then have a beautiful IJssel boulevard where people can walk, cycle, or enjoy recreation in other ways.

112233.explanation: The Christian Union sees the added value of culture for society and for the attractiveness of the city. We prefer culture in its broad sense. Museum Eicas attracts a limited (elitist) audience and knew from the start that they could not rely on financial support from the municipality.

112175.explanation: Gender should not determine whether someone is suitable for a position. However, it is important to look at a good balance in diversity within the council.

112179.explanation: As long as these do not harm the health of our residents, for example by purchasing pallet stoves, we have no objections.

112181.explanation: If you want to prevent litter, you have to do this. The discussion about bulky waste has been going on for years but keeps finding no solution. Therefore, this could be one of the solutions. Raising the weight limit for bulky waste at the recycling center to 500 kilos or more is also, in our opinion, a solution.

112189.explanation: The safety of our residents should not depend on more or fewer enforcement officers.

112190.explanation: Not all places should have camera surveillance. You could consider cameras being temporarily placed at locations with nuisance. Permanent cameras would be better, in our opinion, at entertainment venues.

112192.explanation: We must leave the monopoly on violence with the police.

112193.explanation: An interest-free starter loan for people who need it is, in our opinion, a good way to help, for example, young people get a home.

112194.explanation: Many of our young people are the victims of the housing crisis. By giving them more rights, they will be more likely to qualify for a home in their own city. This also prevents many talented people from living outside Deventer when they do not want to.

112198.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits should focus on getting out of it as quickly as possible. It should be a temporary arrangement for people who may need retraining for other work. Residents who cannot find work often also do volunteer work.

112200.explanation: We find that people who just earn slightly above a higher income threshold often miss out on various schemes. Therefore, raising this limit could be a solution.

112206.explanation: Culturally sensitive regulations should, in our opinion, also receive more attention.

112208.explanation: This need among our residents is strongly present. That is why we have advocated combating period poverty in Deventer.

112211.explanation: A good source of income for the municipality. This should be combined with improving facilities for tourists.

112212.explanation: More business parks mean more employment and more support for the economy.

112214.explanation: There must be a good balance in the flow of people who have obtained a residence status. A clear rule should be applied to make this process as fair as possible.

112215.explanation: In most cases, this is handled by the province.

112217.explanation: We should not interfere with the educational offering. This should be entirely the responsibility of the primary schools themselves. What constitutes 'Deventers' is also, in our view, a question.

112218.explanation: As mentioned earlier, we want a deterrent policy on drug use.

112222.explanation: We must prevent aggressive behavior by drivers as much as possible. Making parking more expensive does not solve making the center accessible but drives away our residents. As long as public transport is not affordable and properly organized in Deventer, you cannot demand this.

112223.explanation: Many neighborhoods struggle with parking problems. The solutions provided by our residents must be taken seriously.

112225.explanation: The reception of asylum seekers is not arranged by the municipality. We follow the advice of national politics. Nevertheless, we do not lose our humanity; no one chooses to flee their own country. The world belongs to all of us.

112226.explanation: Merging these parties will drive away many artists in the future because the spaces a pop venue needs are not the solution here. The atmosphere of both important cultural providers will also change.

112227.explanation: With the growth of Deventer, we should not limit ourselves to a number of homes but strive for the most efficient housing supply.

112228.explanation: Support for art is certainly important.

112229.explanation: In consultation with the municipality of Voorst, this would be a great solution to promote flow. A possible first tunnel under the IJssel should also be investigated.

112230.explanation: Go Ahead Eagles has always been a reliable partner and as long as this remains so, they should receive support from the municipality. Football unites the city, which we as DENK consider an important factor.

112231.explanation: There must be a deterrent policy on drugs. Facilitating it does not help addiction care. Something that harms our health should not be supported.

112232.explanation: Without a good solution for the flow of our traffic, it is impossible to start the discussion now. Many roads are already being closed or made one-way.

112233.explanation: Where necessary, we must support our cultural providers.

112175.explanation: The number of women in political positions still lags behind. It would be nice if we also got more diversity in the municipal government in Deventer.

112179.explanation: Burning causes complaints among people with vulnerable health. A ban is difficult to enforce, and a national ban does not yet exist. There should be good information and agreements about when burning should not happen, especially during poor air quality (calm/misty weather).

112181.explanation: We have been advocating for years for a trial with bulky waste collection. For example, periodically placing a container in the neighborhood. This prevents illegal dumping at the hotspots and thus is also a saving for the municipality.

112189.explanation: The number of enforcement officers in our municipality is currently too low, and therefore tasks are left undone. More deployment in nuisance areas can increase the feeling of safety. Tackling problems requires not only enforcement but also more involvement from neighborhood and youth work.

112190.explanation: Surveillance cameras should only be installed in specific cases when all other options have been tried.

112192.explanation: Enforcement officers, like other emergency workers, increasingly face serious violence and lack self-protection equipment. A baton could be an option for this.

112193.explanation: With this form of support, we drive up housing prices. It is also up to banks, not the municipality, to provide loans.

112194.explanation: For crucial professions, the municipality should look at priority in the housing market.

112198.explanation: This should take into account people's personal situations and also any tasks they already perform, such as caregiving or volunteer work.

112200.explanation: The number of working poor (people just above the minimum income) is increasing. This group often falls just outside the income threshold. As a result, poverty remains hidden and problems increase. Late assistance is often more expensive assistance. Therefore, raising the income threshold is a better and cheaper option.

112206.explanation: Freedom of choice in care and support is very important. That is why it is also important that Deventer continues to work with a broad range of care services and that the right to a personal budget (PGB) is maintained.

112208.explanation: Fortunately, awareness of poverty issues is increasing. Period poverty is one of the problems. Distribution of sanitary pads is necessary. The real problem, of course, is the increasing poverty.

112209.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in Deventer rises along with inflation and is therefore not extremely high. In addition, the ozb is also one of the few sources of income for the municipality.

112211.explanation: Deventer owes much to tourism. Any tax increase must be proportional.

112212.explanation: There are currently sufficient business parks in Deventer. Redevelopment of existing sites takes precedence over new developments. Furthermore, priority should be given to supporting local businesses. We have enough large block buildings.

112214.explanation: As long as national legislation (the dispersion law) remains in effect, the municipality is obliged to implement this policy. Fortunately, the housing corporations in our municipality manage to carry this out in a balanced way.

112215.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs, not on agricultural land. The municipality should focus more on using solar panels at, for example, parking lots and noise barriers.

112217.explanation: Municipalities should not interfere with the content of education. Schools can always choose to pay attention to this themselves.

112218.explanation: The current number meets demand in the short term. For the future, there must remain room for expansion.

112222.explanation: Paid parking is already quite expensive in our municipality. However, street parking should be more expensive than parking in garages.

112223.explanation: The needs of residents must be considered. If the extra parking spaces are only used by people from outside the neighborhood or commuters, residents gain little from it.

112225.explanation: Deventer already carries a large responsibility in sheltering asylum seekers. It is now up to other municipalities in our region to also take responsibility. Temporary extra shelter can only be considered in cases of real emergency or force majeure.

112226.explanation: The Burgerweeshuis is the local pop venue, with its own visitors and a young audience. Merging with the Theater is driven by budget cuts and is therefore not a fully adequate alternative to the current location.

112227.explanation: The number of homes that must be built to meet demand is much greater than 11,000. The municipality must invest more in affordable and suitable housing.

112228.explanation: Permanent workplaces for artists are needed to strengthen our appeal as a municipality. These workplaces are a breeding ground for creativity and artists also offer residents the opportunity to become acquainted with art and culture in an accessible way.

112229.explanation: The municipality must invest more in better public transport.

112230.explanation: The municipality can support Go Ahead Eagles by providing a loan. All other forms of financial support are considered state aid and cannot be given.

112231.explanation: When considering a new coffee shop, locations outside the center can also be taken into account.

112232.explanation: Making the area car-free is not feasible. Making it low-traffic is an option that can be explored in the coming period.

112233.explanation: A large cultural offering is important for the attraction and settlement policy in our municipality. Museum Eicas is an added value for our municipality. However, municipal support can only be provided under certain subsidy conditions.

112175.explanation: We assess candidates based on their qualities, not their gender.

112179.explanation: The SGP opposes a ban on wood burning. It is important that neighbors consider each other. The burning guide can help with this.

112181.explanation: Residents can bring bulky waste to the dump for free. Separate collection of bulky waste is too expensive.

112189.explanation: The SGP considers street enforcement important, but community officers (boa’s) should not replace the police.

112190.explanation: In combating crime in business parks, more use can be made of cameras. However, we do not want cameras in pedestrian areas or sidewalks and certainly not with facial recognition.

112192.explanation: Community officers (boa’s) should not take over police tasks. However, the SGP believes boa’s should be able to carry a baton if necessary for their safety and to perform their work well.

112193.explanation: The SGP supports the use of starter loans to keep the municipality of Deventer attractive for young people. However, the SGP believes this must be done financially responsibly and together with sufficient housing construction and flow.

112194.explanation: Priority may be given to local residents so that young people and families can continue living in their own neighborhood or village. This is especially important in the villages and hamlets.

112198.explanation: People who cannot find paid employment can contribute a lot through volunteer work. Encouraging this is good. However, making it mandatory could put people who are unable to volunteer in a difficult position.

112200.explanation: If the income threshold is raised, more people receive help, but those just above the threshold have less left. The focus is on help with debts and supporting people who want to work more.

112206.explanation: It builds trust to receive care from someone who understands you and shares your values.

112208.explanation: There are several places in Deventer where women and girls can get free tampons or sanitary pads. The municipality already supports this and does not need to take it over itself.

112209.explanation: The SGP stands for reliable financial policy. Reducing burdens is not possible without cuts.

112211.explanation: Deventer has a relatively low tourist tax. That fits a hospitable city, so it only needs to rise if necessary.

112212.explanation: If more homes are built in Deventer, more employment is also needed. We want to make room for that.

112214.explanation: The SGP believes refugees should not automatically get priority for social housing, as starters often wait a long time. They should be placed fairly on the waiting list.

112215.explanation: The SGP wants to protect agriculture and prefers solar panels mainly on roofs. A complete ban is not the right measure: existing panels do not need to be removed. Panels on private property are also still allowed.

112217.explanation: Attention to local language and culture strengthens the connection with Deventer. It should not become mandatory; schools should have freedom.

112218.explanation: The SGP does not want new coffee shops and calls for better supervision to prevent hard drug trade by customers at coffee shops.

112222.explanation: The SGP prefers a different solution: a park-and-ride near the A1 with inexpensive parking and good public transport.

112223.explanation: There must be sufficient parking for residents, which is often a problem. We should especially look at how to do this efficiently without cutting down all the trees.

112225.explanation: The SGP is critical of the number of asylum seekers in the Netherlands. During the assessment of their application, they need a safe and livable place. Deventer has enough shelter places and we want to keep it that way.

112226.explanation: We certainly do not want a yo-yo policy. If merging leads to lower costs, that creates space to strengthen other cultural facilities.

112227.explanation: There is a high demand for new homes and too many rules delay construction. However, we must critically examine where and how building takes place.

112228.explanation: The SGP is cautious about investing in this. Government money should primarily go to core tasks (care, safety, housing). We do support the use of vacant buildings and give space and appreciation for initiatives from society.

112229.explanation: Deventer needs a third bridge to reduce traffic jams around the center and to handle growth in traffic. For that, we do need municipality Voorst; otherwise, it won’t work.

112230.explanation: Professional football can raise enough money without subsidies. In the past, loans were taken and properly repaid, but even when borrowing, conditions are necessary.

112231.explanation: SGP prefers no new coffeeshops at all, neither inside nor outside the center.

112232.explanation: We mainly want to relieve the Welle by building a park-and-ride and a third bridge. The SGP also supports lowering speed limits to improve safety.

112233.explanation: The SGP is cautious about investing in this. Government money should primarily be spent on core tasks (care, safety, housing).

112175.explanation: We stand for an inclusive and diverse municipal government that reflects society fairly, so it aims for an equal male/female distribution in the executive board.

112179.explanation: We want to tackle wood-burning because of fine dust and nuisance. Wood-burning in Deventer will be phased out to improve air quality.

112181.explanation: Collecting or dropping off bulky waste is free up to a certain amount. Residents who produce less waste pay less. Littering and adding extra waste are actively combated.

112189.explanation: There will be more enforcement of animal rights, nature, and environmental issues, such as illegal fireworks discharge. Enforcement officers do not engage in ethnic profiling or stigmatization.

112190.explanation: Camera surveillance is only allowed temporarily in certain areas. Camera surveillance offers only apparent security and often just shifts the problem elsewhere.

112192.explanation: There will be more focus on the causes of crime and prevention, for example by investing in youth in disadvantaged situations. The municipality also promotes prevention through good and meaningful youth work and enough community workers.

112193.explanation: Housing is a basic right. We help starters with sufficient suitable housing. It is questionable whether the starter’s loan is the right instrument for this.

112194.explanation: When demolishing and rebuilding a neighborhood, at least the same number of social rental homes must be rebuilt to prevent displacement of people with lower incomes (gentrification).

112198.explanation: We oppose a mandatory counter-performance. Volunteer work and informal care are supported but not made compulsory; the benefit recipient themselves is central, not the free provision of labor.

112200.explanation: The income limit for poverty schemes should be increased to 130% of the social minimum. This would allow more low-income people to qualify for support.

112206.explanation: We value tailored care. Care providers and shelters must take clients' backgrounds, identities, and lifestyles into account so everyone feels safe and understood in care.

112208.explanation: The municipality supports initiatives that make menstrual products available. We expand municipal facilities for people with low incomes.

112209.explanation: Deventer continues to invest in people, animals, and nature; lowering the property tax (ozb) would undermine this care and ambition.

112211.explanation: Tourist tax is a useful levy to finance sustainability. The proceeds must be used to limit the damage tourism causes to climate and nature.

112212.explanation: We choose to make good use of existing space, preferably on existing sites. Preferably sustainable and clean industry. If an industrial estate is established, an equal amount of greenery will be planted.

112214.explanation: We continue with the current policy to house status holders.

112215.explanation: We follow the solar ladder: first roofs, then for example roadside verges, and only as a last option agricultural land. Agricultural land is unsuitable for large-scale solar parks; that land is meant for food production, nature, or possibly housing after livestock farming shrinks.

112217.explanation: We leave this up to the primary schools.

112218.explanation: Using and producing soft drugs is allowed locally and on a small scale. There will be information about the dangers of (soft) drugs. No new coffeeshops near schools. Deventer is open to trials with organic local cannabis cultivation.

112222.explanation: Preferably no parking at all in the inner city and replaced by cheap parking outside the city combined with public transport.

112223.explanation: We promote public transport and reduce car use. Therefore, no extra parking spaces.

112225.explanation: The municipality of Deventer welcomes refugees. We believe Deventer should continue with sheltering. We focus on small-scale, humane shelter within the municipality, not on limiting numbers.

112226.explanation: We have strong doubts about the added value of merging and oppose financing buildings. We prefer to invest in creators and culture.

112227.explanation: We support creating housing in ways other than new construction, for example by converting vacant offices. Growth of 11,000 homes is too much. That would make Deventer grow by a quarter. Facilities must grow along with it.

112228.explanation: The budget for art and culture will be structurally increased to program more local talent and to implement the Fair Practice Code (for fair pay in the cultural sector).

112229.explanation: We promote public transport and reduce car use. This way we make a third IJssel bridge unnecessary.

112230.explanation: That is possible in the form of a loan.

112231.explanation: Using and producing soft drugs is allowed locally and on a small scale. There will be information about the dangers of (soft) drugs. No new coffeeshops near schools.

112232.explanation: The Welle becomes car-free and transforms into a green, lively boulevard. This connects the inner city with the IJssel and creates space for walking, cycling, and socializing.

112233.explanation: The budget for arts and culture will be structurally increased.

112175.explanation: Volt chooses a balanced distribution of men and women in the municipal executive board. Equal representation strengthens decisions and trust. Diversity leads to better choices for all residents of Deventer and ensures everyone feels seen. Together we build inclusive governance.

112179.explanation: Volt limits wood burning to keep the air cleaner and healthier. Fireplaces and wood stoves cause nuisance and harmful emissions in residential areas. We encourage cleaner alternatives and assist people in making the switch. This way, we protect health and the living environment together.

112181.explanation: Volt opts for paid bulky waste collection. A contribution encourages reuse and repair, resulting in less waste thrown away. People who really cannot afford this are supported. Together we reduce waste and strengthen the circular economy in our municipality.

112189.explanation: Volt focuses on safe neighborhoods by investing mainly in prevention, youth work, and strong neighborhood networks. Enforcement remains a supplement, not the first measure. Good lighting, opportunities for social encounters, and swift signaling prevent problems. In this way, we increase safety together with residents.

112190.explanation: Volt prioritizes safety with respect for privacy. More cameras are not the standard solution. We improve lighting, design streets to be clear and orderly, and strengthen social cohesion. Where necessary, we implement targeted measures. This way, we create safe neighborhoods based on trust.

112192.explanation: Volt chooses community enforcement officers (boa’s) without batons. The focus is on contact with residents, de-escalation, and cooperation with the police. Training, good communication, and visible presence enhance safety. This keeps things humane and maintains strong public trust in public spaces.

112193.explanation: Volt does not support starter loans. Additional borrowing capacity drives up house prices further and ultimately does not help first-time buyers. The solution lies in more affordable homes and sufficient social rental housing. This way, we create real opportunities for starters to find a home in Deventer.
