112194.explanation: Volt does not give priority based on municipal borders. Allocation happens based on need and urgency, not postcode. This keeps the system fair and people-centered. We build more affordable housing so waiting times decrease. Everyone deserves a chance at suitable housing.

112198.explanation: Volt chooses trust over coercion. People on social assistance already make great efforts to get by. Mandatory obligations increase stress and rarely offer sustainable solutions. We focus on guidance, education, and health. This way, everyone participates in a way that truly suits them.

112200.explanation: Volt raises the income threshold for poverty support. More and more people struggle to make ends meet, even with work. With broader access to assistance, we prevent debt and social exclusion. This strengthens financial security and ensures everyone can participate in daily life.

112206.explanation: Volt prioritizes quality and accessibility of care, not selection based on religion or culture. Everyone receives good care nearby and without barriers. Too strong a focus on separate target groups reduces the overall care offer and makes access more difficult for others. This way, care remains available for everyone.

112208.explanation: Volt acknowledges that period poverty is a serious problem. Targeted support for people with low incomes is more effective than general free distribution. Through cooperation between schools, healthcare, and neighborhood teams, we lower barriers and ensure no one is without basic products.

112209.explanation: Volt does not lower property tax. Strong services such as sports, greenery, youth care, and culture require solid income. We choose transparency about spending and involve residents in decisions. This keeps Deventer livable and future-proof for everyone.

112211.explanation: Volt raises the tourist tax. Tourism also brings crowds and extra costs. The revenue is used for maintenance of facilities, clean streets, and spreading visitors across city and villages. This keeps Deventer livable and hospitable for all.

112212.explanation: Volt does not choose to create more new industrial sites. First, we use and make existing sites more sustainable. The A1 area has a strong focus on circular economy and innovation. Large-scale, non-sustainable industry does not fit here. This way, we grow within our limits.

112214.explanation: Volt gives priority to refugees with a residence permit where necessary for social housing. Successful integration starts with a stable living situation. Guidance towards language, education, and participation provides perspective within a year and a feeling of home. In this way, mutual understanding grows in Deventer.

112215.explanation: Volt makes Deventer an energy pilot city. Local energy generation is essential to become energy neutral. Solar panels on agricultural land can fit within sustainable farming, with attention to nature and landscape. Together with residents, we speed up the transition.

112217.explanation: Volt supports attention to the Deventer dialect in all primary schools. Local culture and dialect strengthen identity, language skills, and connection between generations. Through stories, music, and lessons about the city, children discover their environment and history in a positive way.

112218.explanation: Volt permits new coffeeshops within clear frameworks. A regulated supply reduces illegal trade and increases safety and oversight. Distribution across the city prevents concentration of nuisance. This creates a sober and effective drug policy that works for residents and entrepreneurs.

112222.explanation: Volt opts for higher paid parking fees in the city center. This stimulates use of bicycles, public transport, and park & ride facilities, keeping the inner city accessible. Less traffic searching for parking creates space for pedestrians and cyclists. This results in a cleaner, safer, and more pleasant city center for residents and visitors.

112223.explanation: Volt does not support increasing parking spaces in residential areas. Additional spots solve the problem temporarily and come at the expense of green and play areas. We improve alternatives like shared mobility, cycling, and public transport. This reduces parking pressure and keeps neighborhoods livable and safe.

112225.explanation: Volt maintains asylum seeker reception levels. Solidarity and humanity are central. A stable home and guidance toward language, education, and participation provide perspective within a year. This enables newcomers to participate and fosters support in society.

112226.explanation: Volt clearly opposes the merger of the Burgerweeshuis and Schouwburg. This is a poor decision that harms diversity and talent development. We strengthen the Burgerweeshuis as a fully-fledged pop venue with the space and resources it deserves. This keeps culture broad and vibrant.

112227.explanation: Volt does not set a maximum of 11,000 new homes. That number is an important milestone, but the housing shortage requires flexibility. We build sustainably and within the carrying capacity of the city and surroundings. This way, we create affordable homes for starters, families, and seniors.

112228.explanation: Volt invests in more affordable workspaces for artists. Empty buildings will be transformed into creative hubs where creators and entrepreneurs meet. This helps retain talent in the city, enriches public spaces, and stimulates innovation and the local economy.

112229.explanation: Volt does not support a third IJssel bridge. Large new infrastructure is costly and attracts more car traffic. Priority lies with better public transport: expansion of bus lines, research into a tram connection, and new train stations in Diepenveen and Bathmen. This way, we improve accessibility sustainably.

112230.explanation: Volt sees the GAE stadium as an important facility for the entire city. Sport connects generations, unites neighborhoods, and offers youth perspectives. Maintaining and renewing sports facilities are part of a vital, proud, and healthy municipality where everyone can participate.

112231.explanation: Volt permits coffeeshops outside the city center as well. Distribution reduces pressure and nuisance in one location and makes facilities more accessible. With clear rules around health, youth, and safety, a sober and effective drug policy emerges that works for the entire city.

112232.explanation: Volt makes the Welle an auto-free zone. Less car traffic creates peace, safety, and cleaner air along the IJssel river. The freed space will be used for walking, cycling, greenery, and leisure by the water. This results in an attractive and livable riverside boulevard for everyone.

112233.explanation: Volt structurally supports Museum EICAS as part of a strong cultural network. Accessible museums and art institutions enhance participation in culture and attract visitors to the city. This strengthens education, creativity, and vibrancy in Deventer.

statements.title: The municipality should financially support Go Ahead Eagles in renovating the stadium.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet Go Ahead Eagles

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: ondersteunen bij het verbouwen van het stadion.

statements.title: In Deventer, the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Deventer moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Deventer should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: A third bridge over the IJssel should be built.
Deventer should have more business parks.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.theme: Civic orphanage and theatre under one roof
statements.title: Deventer should refrain from merging Het Burgerweeshuis and the Schouwburg under one roof.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Deventer.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The number of asylum seekers that Deventer accommodates should remain the same.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: dat Deventer opvangt moet gelijk blijven.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
No more than 11,000 new homes may be built.
Paid parking in the city center should become more expensive.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Deventer should invest more money in workspaces for artists.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Deventer should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Deventer controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: De Welle should become car-free.
The municipality should enable all primary schools to give attention to the Deventer dialect.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Deventer should provide ongoing financial support to museum Eicas.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Deventer should also allow coffeeshops outside the city center.

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).
